{"problem_standardized_math":"Compute the minimum-norm solution of the system\n\\[\nr_e=A_e(x)v_x+c_e(x),\\qquad r_I=A_I(x)v_x-Sv_s+(c_I(x)-s).\n\\]\n(This system defines the Newton component in the dogleg method used to find an approximate solution to\n\\[\n\\min_v \\|A_e(x)v_x+c_e(x)\\|_2^2+\\|A_I(x)v_x-Sv_s+(c_I(x)-s)\\|_2^2\n\\]\nsubject to\n\\[\n\\|(v_x,v_s)\\|_2\\le 0.8\\Delta,\\qquad v_s\\ge -(\\tau/2)e.\n\\]) Show that the computation of this Newton component can use the factorization of the augmented matrix\n\\[\n\\begin{bmatrix}\nI & \\hat A^T \\\\\n\\hat A & 0\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad \\text{where} \\qquad\n\\hat A=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\nA_E(x) & 0 \\\\\nA_I(x) & -S\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]"}
